sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Abdominal Pain in Adults – 11
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for adult Dubai telehealth abdominal-pain documentation; partial where the baseline workflow has no adult-only filter or telehealth restriction.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines adult abdominal-pain use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Recommendation — clinical history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies onset, duration, location, character, severity, associated gastrointestinal, urinary, gynaecological, exposure, travel, trauma, and medical-history domains.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists sudden or severe pain, fever, vomiting, rigid abdomen, bleeding, weight loss, dysphagia, haematuria, pregnancy, and shock-associated features.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists blood tests, urinalysis, pregnancy testing, pancreatic enzymes, and imaging selected according to the clinical assessment.

exact_sections.heading: 11. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists specialist and emergency referral criteria for adult abdominal pain.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Chest Pain – 45
sources.population: People presenting with chest pain; no precise age range is stated in the applicability section.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth chest-pain documentation; partial for a setting-agnostic GP workflow and emergency presentations excluded from telehealth.

exact_sections.heading: 1. Background; 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines chest-pain scope in DHA telehealth and emphasises potentially life-threatening causes and emergency exclusion.

exact_sections.heading: 5. History Taking/Symptoms
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies onset, site, character, radiation, severity, exertional and relieving factors, associated symptoms, duration, trauma, travel, and prior testing.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Red Flags and Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists life-threatening chest-pain patterns, associated breathlessness, syncope, diaphoresis, abnormal physiology, and emergency referral requirements.

exact_sections.heading: 10. Investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Limits testing to the assessed presentation and lists blood studies, chest radiography, ECG, and echocardiography where clinically indicated.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Common Cold
sources.population: Adults and children with common cold; severity caveats for neonates, older people, and long-term conditions.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for common-cold telehealth documentation; only partial for the broader fever/URTI baseline workflow.

exact_sections.heading: 1. Background
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Identifies severity-risk contexts and distinguishes uncomplicated common cold from other respiratory presentations.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth scope and emergency/controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists respiratory, swallowing, neurologic, rash, and pediatric breathing concerns that require assessment or escalation.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Constipation – 04
sources.population: People presenting with constipation; no precise age range is stated in the applicability section.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth constipation documentation; partial for a setting-agnostic GP workflow.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Recommendation — constipation history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies nature, onset, duration, bowel diary, secondary causes, straining, incomplete evacuation, abdominal pain, bleeding, and medication context.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists new onset in older people, anaemia, rectal bleeding, family history, tenesmus, weight loss, and persistent unresponsive constipation.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Limits laboratory and colorectal imaging evaluation to persistent, unresponsive, or suspected secondary disease.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists emergency bleeding and specialist referral criteria for constipation.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dizziness
sources.population: People presenting with dizziness; age limits are not stated in the applicability section.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth dizziness scope and emergency/controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5.1 Virtual Clinical Assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Emphasises structured history and available examination to distinguish dizziness syndromes.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists cardiac, neurologic, trauma, intoxication, visual, consciousness, and severe-headache concerns.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Fatigue – 06
sources.population: People presenting with fatigue; no precise age range is stated in the applicability section.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth fatigue documentation; partial for a setting-agnostic GP workflow.

exact_sections.heading: 1. Background
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines fatigue domains, duration categories, and broad medical, psychological, medication, and substance-related associations.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA telehealth scope, Dubai purpose, licensed-provider applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Recommendation — history and associated symptoms
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies fatigue description, onset, course, duration, functional effect, sleep, mood, associated symptoms, medication, family, and social history.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists weight loss, bleeding, breathlessness, fever, neurological progression, malignancy features, and suicidal tendency as concern prompts.

exact_sections.heading: 8. Investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists initial laboratory investigations for subacute or chronic fatigue and limits additional testing to the evaluated history.

exact_sections.heading: 10. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists emergency and face-to-face referral criteria for fatigue presentations.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Fever in Children
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for pediatric fever telehealth documentation; partial for a mixed-age fever/URTI workflow.

exact_sections.heading: 1. Background
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines pediatric fever assessment context and the importance of age, measurement site, appearance, persistence, and underlying disease.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth pediatric fever scope and emergency exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5.1 Virtual Clinical Assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for adult Dubai telehealth gastroenteritis documentation; partial for a broad diarrhea workflow without an adult or acute-duration filter.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines adult gastroenteritis use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Recommendation — clinical manifestations and history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines acute diarrheal illness and records stool frequency, duration, nausea, vomiting, fever, abdominal pain, exposure pattern, and dehydration features.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists shock, severe dehydration, intractable vomiting, severe abdominal pain, prolonged symptoms, older age, comorbidity, pregnancy, antibiotic exposure, nocturnal diarrhea, and bloody or black stool.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Investigation/Diagnosis
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes history-based assessment and investigation for suspected nonviral or complicated gastroenteritis.

exact_sections.heading: 10. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines adult headache telehealth scope in DHA-licensed facilities and emergency exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Red Flags

exact_sections.heading: 8. Emergency Evaluation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Identifies headache presentations requiring urgent face-to-face assessment.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Hypertension – 27
sources.population: Adults with suspected or established hypertension.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth hypertension assessment and follow-up; partial for a general setting-agnostic follow-up workflow.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth hypertension scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Recommendation — blood-pressure assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies office, repeated home, and ambulatory blood-pressure assessment and hypertension staging.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Management — monitoring and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes monitoring phases, clinician-judged laboratory review, lifestyle domains, and monthly review context.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Referral Criteria and Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists severe blood pressure, confusion, visual changes, chest pain, breathlessness, neurological symptoms, severe headache, and specialist referral criteria.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 1 — tests required for hypertension
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists follow-up electrolytes, renal function, lipids, urine ACR, glucose or HbA1c, liver tests, blood count, and ECG according to medication and result context.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Nausea and Vomiting – 08
sources.population: People presenting with nausea or vomiting; no precise age range is stated in the applicability section.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth nausea and vomiting documentation; partial for a general in-person GP workflow.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Recommendation — virtual clinical assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies distinction between nausea, vomiting, retching, and regurgitation and records duration, frequency, associated symptoms, intake, and likely causes.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists severe abdominal pain, gastrointestinal bleeding, neurological symptoms, chest pain, weight loss, dehydration, altered state, and metabolic concerns.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Investigations

exact_sections.heading: 10. Referral Criteria

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth scope and exclusions for emergency and controlled-medication prescribing.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30
sources.population: Adults with type 2 diabetes; specific diagnostic exclusions are stated for children, pregnancy, and selected conditions.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for adult Dubai telehealth type 2 diabetes review; partial because the baseline workflow has no explicit adult-only filter and is not limited to telehealth.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth type 2 diabetes scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5.6 Annual/6 monthly review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Referral Criteria and Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists metabolic decompensation, DKA or hyperosmolar state, infected foot ulcer, profound hypoglycaemia, and rapidly deteriorating complications.

exact_sections.heading: 7.2 Refilling/Prescribing Diabetic Medication — monitoring prerequisites
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies clinician review of BP, creatinine, electrolytes, HbA1c, cholesterol, urine ACR, home glucose context, and adherence before medication decisions.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 7 — summary of required laboratory tests
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Summarises HbA1c, renal, lipid, ALT, and urine ACR review intervals for diabetes.
